Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our FiIe No. I-9 0-08 2
Dear Mr. Almanza 3
You have requested advice regarding the duties of menbers o!
the connission for Economic Development, and special task forces
created ttrereof , under the confliq! of interest provisions of the
political 
Reform Act (the rras;t'1.1/ Your letter does not concern
i sleciric governmentil decision; therefore, we are considering it
to te a request for informal assistance.z/
OUESTTONS
Are Dembers of the commission for Economic Development (the
rcornmissiontt) subject to the Act's conflict of interest
p.""i-i"n=e 'If s6, should their special task force appointees
also report?
CONCITUSION
The members and special task force appointees of the
conmission are rtpublic officials" 
as defined in the Act requiring

FACTS
The commission was created to provide continuing bipartisan
legislative, 
executive branch and private sector support and
guidance for the best possible overall economic developrnent of the
state .
The comnission is comprised of 17 members. Members consist
of the Lieutenant covernor, three Senators, three Assenbly nenbers
and ten public menbers appointed by the Governor from business,
industry and labor organizations.
Members serve rrithout compensation from the cornmission, but
shal1 be reimbursed for actual necessary expenses incurred in the
perfornance of their duties, as authorized by the commission
chairman.MembersoftheSenateandAsSemb1ysha].1receive>
reimbursement frorn their legislative funds onIy.
The cornmission may appoint task forces or advisory cornmittees
to study and report on specific issues $rhich relate to their
purposes. The commission nay contract for studies and other
special services.
ANALYST S
Conflict of interest codes are required to identify
rrdesignated employeesrr and to require that those designated
positions file appropriate financial disclosure statements.
lSection A73O2.) Section 82019 provides that a "designated
employeerr is an officer, employee, member, or consultant of any
agency r,rhose position with the. agency .. ' who is involved as a
state ernployeL at other than a clerical or ministerial fevel in
the functions of negotiating or signing any contract awarded
through cornpetitive bidding, in naking decisions in conjunction
with the competitive bidding process′
 or ■n neqotiating, s■
on■nq′
10122 of the Public contract code.rr (Ernphasis added. )
A public official or designated employee "participates in the
naking of a qovernrnental decisionr' lrhen, acting within the
authority of his or her positj.on, he or she3
(1) Negotiates, without significant substantive review, with
a governmental entity or private person regarding the
decision; or
(2) Advises or nakes recommendations to the decision-maker,
either directly or without significant intervening
substantive review, bY:
(A) conducting research or making any investigation
which requires the exercise of judgrment on the part
‐
|


File No. I-90-082
Page 3
of the official 
or designated employee and the
purpose of which is to influence the decision, or
(B) Preparing or presenting any report, analysis or
opinion, orally or in writing, which requires the
exercise of judgrtent on the part of the official 
or
designated employee and the purpose of which is to
influence the decision.
Regulation 187o0(c) (1) and (c) (2).
The term trdesignated employeetr does not include any
unsalaried member of a board or cornmission which perforns a soJ-ely
advisory function.
Acommissionpossessesdecisionmakingauthorityif:>
(A) It nay make a final governmental decision;
(B) ft nay compel a governmental decision; or it may prevent
a governmental decision by reason of an excLusive power to
initiate the decision or by reason of a veto which may not be
overriddenl or
(c) It makes substantive recommendations which are, and over
an extended period of time have been, regularly apProved without
significant lmendment or nodification by another public official
or governmental agency. (section 82019.)
The commission will be considering programs to further the
econonic development of the state, contracting for and conducting
studies of laws and programs oi other states relating to economic
development and the encouragenent of business and industry, and
sha11 -confer with interested persons or parties concerned with the
prornotion of economic development. The commission shall nake
iecornmendations concerning lLgislation affecting the economic
development of the state to the Governor and the Legislature
annua1ly.
Based upon the facts which you have provided us,, it is our
conclusion that the nenbers of the comnissi.on and nembers of its
speciat task forces have decision-making authority, and- thus
sirould be designated in a conflict of interest code' Therefore,
they will be required to subrnit disclosure statements' However,
it you have additional documentation which indicates that the
rec'ommendations made by the commission are not routinely approved,
Lr that they receive slgnificant intervening substantive reviev by
the Legisl,alrrre, *" nay rrish to reevaluate our advice'
